# GPU Pricing — Live Platform Rates

Prices set by supply and demand across 40+ data centers. On-demand, interruptible, or reserved — find the right GPU at the right price.

Most Popular

* Per-second billing

* Spin up/down anytime

### Interruptible

Best Value

50%+ cheaper. Best for batch training.

* Preemptible — may be reclaimed
* Ideal for fault-tolerant workloads
* Checkpoint and resume easily

### Reserved

Up to 50% Off

Long-term commitment. Best for steady workloads.
* 1, 3, or 6 month terms
* Guaranteed capacity
* Volume discounts available

Contact Sales

Estimate your GPU costs by model, count, and usage hours.

## Flagship GPU Pricing

Live price distribution across the platform. Updated in real time.

## Why Vast  
Pricing Works

A GPU platform means real competition, real transparency, and structurally lower prices.

Contact Sales Team

### Supply & Demand Pricing

Prices are set by the market, not by Vast. More supply means lower prices — and you always see the real rate.

### Per-Second Billing

Pay only for what you use, down to the second. No minimum hours, no rounding up, no surprise charges.

### 68+ GPU Types

From RTX 3060 to B200 — find exactly the right GPU for your workload and budget across the full spectrum.

### No Lock-In

No long-term contracts required. Scale up, scale down, or switch GPU types anytime without penalties.

Subscribe for our product updates.

results.title: NVIDIA AI GPU Prices: H100 ($27K-$40K) & H200 ($315K/8-GPU) Cost Guide | IntuitionLabs
excerpts:
Meanwhile, the China-only Blackwell GPU ( _RTX Pro 6000D_ ) is around $7K as reported ( [<sup>[12]</sup>](https://www.reuters.com/world/china/nvidia-launch-cheaper-blackwell-ai-chip-china-after-us-export-curbs-sources-say-2025-05-24/#:~:text=Nvidia%20plans%20to%20launch%20a,new%20chip%20features%20reduced%20specifications "Highlights: Nvidia plans to launch a,new chip features reduced specifications") ) – again reflecting its simpler design (GDDR7 memory, no CoWoS).

It is worth noting that **system-level pricing** often embeds multiple GPUs plus CPU, networking, etc. A full DGX A100 system (8×A100) launched in the $200K range ( [<sup>[34]</sup>](https://cyfuture.cloud/blog/nvidia-a100-price-in-2025-a-deep-dive-into-value-specs-and-ai-workloads/#:~:text=,Between%20%24200%2C000%20and%20%24250%2C000 "Highlights: ,Between $200,000 and $250,000") ), a DGX H200 (8×H200) is quoted ~$400–$500K ( [<sup>[3]</sup>](https://www.trgdatacenters.
com/resource/nvidia-dgx-buyers-guide-everything-you-need-to-know/#:~:text=DGX%20H200%20systems%20are%20currently,2026 "Highlights: DGX H200 systems are currently,2026") ), and the newer DGX B200 (8×B200) exceeds $500K while the DGX B300 lists at $300–350K ( [<sup>[11]</sup>](https://tech-insider.org/nvidia-blackwell-gpu-pricing/) ). These capital costs underscore the premium attached to aggregate GPU compute.

# Cloud vs. On-Premise Cost Analysis

Organizations frequently evaluate whether to **buy** GPUs outright or **rent** time in the cloud. Cloud providers (AWS, Google, Azure, etc.) offer GPU instances billed by the hour, while on-premise purchase is a large upfront investment amortized over years. Here we compare these models using published rates and examples.

**Cloud Rental Rates:** Over 2024–25, hyperscalers aggressively cut GPU-hourly rates. For the NVIDIA H100, on-demand pricing by late 2025 had fallen to the low single digits per GPU-hour.
Google Cloud’s A3 (1×H100) was about **$3.00/GPU-hr** , and AWS EC2 P5 (8×H100) about **$3.93/GPU-hr** ( [<sup>[7]</sup>](https://intuitionlabs.ai/articles/h100-rental-prices-cloud-comparison#:~:text=Key%20findings%3A%20The%20cheapest%20current,hour%29%20by%20vendor%20include "Highlights: Key findings: The cheapest current,hour) by vendor include") ). Microsoft Azure’s H100-enabled instances were higher (roughly **$6.98/GPU-hr** , East US) ( [<sup>[7]</sup>](https://intuitionlabs.ai/articles/h100-rental-prices-cloud-comparison#:~:text=Key%20findings%3A%20The%20cheapest%20current,hour%29%20by%20vendor%20include "Highlights: Key findings: The cheapest current,hour) by vendor include") ). Smaller cloud GPU vendors competed harder: e.g. Lambda Labs offered H100 at $2.99/hr, RunPod spot instances at $1.99/hr, and Vast.ai marketplace around $1.87/hr ( [<sup>[35]</sup>](https://intuitionlabs.ai/articles/h100-rental-prices-cloud-comparison#:~:text=demand%20%28in%20East%20US%29.

results.title: 7 cheapest cloud GPU providers in 2026 | Blog - Northflank
excerpts:
Major cloud providers often require quota requests that can take days to approve, while some platforms impose spending limits for new users.

The optimal approach is to find a platform that combines competitive pricing with reliability, transparent billing, and the flexibility to scale with your needs.

## Now, let’s see a quick comparison of the cheapest cloud GPU services

Now that you know what to look for, let's compare how the major platforms perform on pricing and value.

I've analyzed the most popular GPU configurations across different providers to show you the pricing as of September 2025.

Keep in mind that while some platforms show lower hourly rates, the total value depends on the factors we just discussed - reliability, hidden fees, and availability.

|Platform |A100 (40GB & 80GB) |H100 (80GB) |Available GPUs |Spot/Discount Options |**Recommendation** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|**🏆 Northflank** |$1.42/hr, $1.76/hr |H100 80GB: $2.
74/hr |A100 40/80GB, H100, H200, B200, L4, L40S, MI300X |Auto spot orchestration + [BYOC](https://northflank.com/features/bring-your-own-cloud) (Bring your own cloud) + Production reliability |**⭐ BEST VALUE** |
|**VastAI** |$0.50–$0.70/hr, $0.60–$0.80 (dynamic pricing) |From $1.77/hr |A100 40/80GB, H100, RTX 4090, wide variety |Peer-to-peer marketplace pricing |Budget experiments |
|**RunPod** |A100 PCIe 80GB: $1.19/hr (Community), $2.17/hr (Serverless) |$2.79/hr (Community), $3.35/hr (Serverless) |H100, A100 80GB, RTX 4090, L40S, A6000 |Community Cloud + Serverless options |AI-focused workflows |
|**TensorDock** |A100 80GB: $1.63/hr |$2.25/hr |A100, H100, RTX 6000, 3090 |Global marketplace |Custom configurations |
|**AWS** |A100 (40GB): ($32.77/hr for 8x GPUs), A100 (80GB): ($40.96/hr for 8x GPUs) |H100 80GB: ($55.04/hr for 8x GPUs) |H100, A100 40GB, L40S, T4 |Up to 90% with Spot Instances |Enterprise at scale |
|**Lambda Labs** |$1.29/hr, $1.79/hr |H100 80GB: $2.
99/hr |H100, H200, A100 40/80GB, B200 |On-demand, reserved instances |Training when available |
|**Paperspace** |A100 (40GB): $3.09/hr\*\*,\*\* A100 (80GB): $3.18/hr |H100 80GB: $5.95/hr |A100, RTX 6000, 3090 |Limited promotional rates |Development & notebooks |

_Prices are representative and may vary by region and availability_

> Note: The cheapest option isn't always the best value. You need to factor in reliability, ease of use, and hidden costs like data transfer fees.
> 
> 

## Why spot instances and BYOC (Bring your own cloud) can save you thousands

Before we go into detail for each platform, let me explain the two strategies that can dramatically cut your costs:

**Spot instances** are unused GPU capacity that cloud providers sell at massive discounts - often 60-90% off regular prices. They can be interrupted with short notice when demand increases. Read more about it in this [guide](https://northflank.com/blog/what-are-spot-gpus-guide) .

Vast.ai RTX 4090 Price: $0.39/hr Typical (Apr 2026)

# Vast.ai RTX 4090 Price: $0.39/hr Typical (Apr 2026)

Mar 4, 2026

## Vast.ai RTX 4090 Price (April 2026)

Vast.ai RTX 4090 price per hour starts at **$0.29** as of April 2026, with most listings around **$0.39/hr** .

* **Lowest price:** $0.29/hr
* **Typical price:** $0.39/hr
* **High-end hosts:** $0.59/hr

Prices vary by host performance, uptime, and storage options.

|Provider |RTX 4090 Price/hr |Model |Last Updated |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|Vast.ai |$0.29–0.59 |Marketplace (host-set) |Apr 2026 |
|RunPod |$0.34 / $0.69 |Community / Secure |Apr 2026 |
|SynpixCloud |$0.39 |Dedicated (SSH + CUDA) |Apr 2026 |
|Lambda Labs |Not offered |Data center GPUs only |Apr 2026 |

For RunPod-specific pricing, see RTX 4090 Price: $0.34/hr RunPod .
For a dedicated GPU with no interruptions, SynpixCloud RTX 4090 is $0.39/hr.

* * *

## Vast.ai RTX 4090 Price Per Hour (April 2026)

**Typical hourly rate: $0.29–0.31/hr** for interruptible instances. On-demand (non-interruptible) listings range from $0.35–0.50/hr depending on host configuration.

### Why Vast.ai RTX 4090 Prices Vary

Vast.ai is an open marketplace. Individual hosts — hobbyists with spare GPUs, small data centers, crypto miners repurposing hardware — set their own prices. You browse listings, evaluate each host's specs and reliability rating, and rent directly.

This marketplace model produces the lowest prices in the GPU cloud market. Hosts compete against each other, and when supply exceeds demand, prices drop. RTX 4090 interruptible instances regularly appear at $0.29–0.31/hr.

But not every $0.29 listing is equal. The price reflects only the GPU hour — it does not tell you about:

* **Network speed** — A $0.29/hr listing might have 100 Mbps upload. A $0.
40/hr listing might offer 1 Gbps. If your workflow involves uploading datasets or downloading large model outputs, bandwidth differences affect total job time and cost.
* **Host uptime** — Some hosts go offline during peak electricity hours. Others run on consumer-grade hardware with no UPS or redundant power. Vast.ai shows host reliability ratings, but a 95% uptime host will interrupt roughly one in every twenty 10-hour jobs.
* **System specs** — CPU count, RAM, NVMe speed, and PCIe lane configuration vary across hosts. Two RTX 4090 listings at the same price can deliver different performance on CPU-bound preprocessing or data loading.

### Vast.ai RTX 4090 Pricing Tiers

|Listing Type |Typical Price/hr |Availability |Interruption Risk |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|Interruptible (bid) |$0.29–0.31 |High |Host can reclaim GPU |
|On-demand |$0.35–0.50 |Moderate |Low (but host-dependent) |
|High-reliability hosts |$0.40–0.55 |Lower |Lowest on marketplace |
